summary:
Strategy training improves working memory function in older adults through targeted cognitive exercises and combined approaches like Tai Chi or multimodal intervention. Evidence shows significant and sustainable enhancements, supporting its effectiveness against age-related decline despite needing more rigorous study. 

---

(End of Round)

We are asked only about whether strategy training reduces age-related deficits in working memory — so we focus strictly on this point:

Strategy training effectively mitigates age-related declines in working memory among older adults based on consistent observational and interventional studies showing measurable improvement. These benefits persist over time, indicating long-term functional advantages from such trainings.
